events.title: Bee Curious: Up Close & Un-bee-lievable
events.description: Join us in celebration of World Bee Day with scientists from the Negaunee Integrative Research and Keller Science Action Centers! This engaging walk-in session dives into the fascinating world of global and local pollinators, focusing on the un-bee-lievable ones and the role they play in keeping our ecosystems blooming. events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Asian American Pacific Islander Heritage Month Storytime with Chicago Public Library
events.description: Join us in the Crown Family PlayLab for a free, drop-in PlayDate honoring Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander Heritage Month ! Families with 2-6 year olds are invited to a free, drop-in, bilingual storytime session hosted by the Chicago Public Library (CPL). events.title: Armour Seminar: Scott Weidensaul
events.description: At this week's talk, Ornithologist and author Scott Weidensaul discusses his book The Return of the Oystercatcher: Saving Birds to Save the Planet.

events.title: Meet a Scientist: Our Cosmic Origins
events.description: View extraterrestrial materials from the Field Museum's collection and explore how scientists use them to learn more about our cosmic origin story. Meet Philipp R. events.title: Family Night featuring The Pokémon Fossil Museum
events.description: Join us for an evening celebrating the grand opening of the Pokémon Fossil Museum at the Field Museum! events.title: Jewish Botany: Plant Medicine and Magic in the Jewish Diaspora
events.description: Celebrate Jewish American Heritage Month! Come view botanical specimens related to the Jewish diaspora and learn about how healers utilized common household plants throughout Eastern Europe to stave off everyday ailments, as well as protect themselves from curses. events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: James Hutton Turns 300!
events.description: Join us in the Crown Family PlayLab for a free, drop-in PlayDate celebrating the 300th Birthday of James Hutton, the Father of Modern Geology! Families with 2-6 year olds are invited to a free, drop-in, storytime session hosted by the Field Museum Geology Collections team to honor James Hutton, who established theories that impact our geology sciences today.

events.title: James Hutton, the father of Modern Geology, turns 300 years old
events.description: Learn about James Hutton and how angular unconformities teach us about deep time, and how modern processes going on now teach us about the rock cycle. Presenters include Paul Mayer, Fossil Invertebrate Collections Manager, and James Holstein, Geology Collections Manager

events.description: The Field Museum is proud to showcase new sculptures from the collection by Oaxacan artist Avelino Pérez. View our newest acquisitions from Oaxaca and learn about the Field Muse

events.title: PlayLab PlayDate: Juneteenth Celebration
events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate Juneteenth. This PlayDate will feature stories and a craft celebrating the liberation of enslaved Black Americans. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories and more!

events.description: Families with children ages 2–6 are invited to a free, drop-in PlayDate to celebrate fatherly figures in their lives on Father's Day. During this storytime and craft session, there will be stories about Dads and a coloring craft for children to share with their loved ones. Join us for a fun-filled PlayDate with stories, coloring, and more!

events.title: Nectar Feeders vs. Insect Hunters: A Closer Look at Bats
events.description: Celebrate Pollinator Week with scientists from the Gantz Family Collections Center! Bats are often celebrated as important nighttime pollinators, but not all bats play that role.
